name: The Second AI Winter
summary: The second major collapse. Expert systems proved too brittle and expensive to maintain at scale. The LISP machine market collapsed as cheaper general-purpose hardware caught up. Japan's Fifth Generation Project was abandoned without achieving its goals. The Strategic Computing Initiative ended. AI funding dried up again, and once more the field's practitioners avoided the term 'artificial intelligence' in favor of more modest labels.
defining_characteristics: Expert systems couldn't scale — maintaining rule bases became prohibitively expensive
The LISP machine market crashed as general-purpose workstations became powerful enough
Japan's Fifth Generation Project officially ended without meeting its goals
AI researchers rebranded as 'machine learning', 'data mining', or 'computational intelligence'
A quieter, more humble research community emerged, focused on mathematical rigor
